source: SLA Industries -> Mothership species conversion notes

species.name: Human
species.identity: Adaptable generalists. No racial extremes; their edge is flexibility and social access to SLA's systems.
species.fieldPressure: Humans are expected to fill doctrine gaps, improvise, and absorb sponsor demands without complaint.
species.mothershipDescription: Baseline human template with a slight Intellect and Sanity edge reflecting bureaucracy, social resilience, and operational flexibility.
notes: Flexible Mort City baseline. No species stat is pushed hard, which keeps Humans broadly useful across all packages.

specialRules.label: Adaptable
specialRules.summary: Once per session, reroll one Stat check and take the better result.

species.name: Frother
species.identity: Clan-human berserkers born with a physiological gift for combat drugs, violence, and recovery.
species.fieldPressure: Keep them pointed at the problem and away from delicate evidence chains or sponsor-facing civilians.
species.mothershipDescription: Extreme Strength, Combat, and Body with a weak Intellect line and dangerously fragile Sanity. Frothers are terror weapons, not balanced operatives.
notes: High physical ceiling, fragile mind, terrible at precision or reflective tasks.

specialRules.label: Berserk State
specialRules.summary: When failing a Sanity Save, the Frother may enter Berserk instead of gaining Stress: +10 to Combat and Strength checks for the scene, but they must pass a Combat check to avoid attacking the nearest target.

specialRules.label: Drug Metabolism
specialRules.summary: No addiction roll for standard SLA combat drugs. One combat drug per scene can be taken without a Body Save penalty.

species.name: Ebon
species.identity: Mystical, emotionally volatile Ebb users whose greatest strength is disciplined mental focus rather than physical resilience.
species.fieldPressure: They are trusted when the mission needs calm precision, but every Flux spike is watched and every psychic event hits them harder.
species.mothershipDescription: Exceptional Intellect backed by weak Strength and Body. Ebons solve impossible problems through Ebb discipline, not brute force.
notes: Physically frail, emotionally sensitive, and deeply tied to the psychic environment around them.

specialRules.label: Ebb Sensitivity
specialRules.summary: Automatically senses nearby Ebb use within Close Range. Sanity Saves against xenoesoteric or reality-warping events are rolled at Disadvantage.

specialRules.label: Flux Pool
specialRules.summary: Begins with Flux equal to Intellect / 4 with a minimum reserve of 20. Flux recovers at 1 point per hour of rest.

species.name: Brain Waster
species.identity: Violent evolutionary offshoots of the Ebon; hateful, aggressive Ebb users who are more dangerous in a fight than in a briefing room.
species.fieldPressure: Command expects results fast, but collateral panic and the psychic shockwave around them are never far behind.
species.mothershipDescription: A harsher Ebb profile than Ebon: stronger, more aggressive, and more immediately dangerous, but socially and psychically corrosive.
notes: Better fighters than Ebons, worse diplomats, and much harder on the people around them.

specialRules.label: Ebb Aggression
specialRules.summary: Uses the same Flux Pool rules as Ebon, including a minimum reserve of 20. When dealing damage with Ebb abilities, add +1d5 damage.

specialRules.label: Unnerving Presence
specialRules.summary: Close-range friendly operatives roll Fear Saves at Disadvantage when the Brain Waster uses an Ebb ability.

species.name: Wraith Raider
species.identity: Feline ice-world hunters with lightning reflexes, superior senses, and the fastest natural movement profile in the World of Progress.
species.fieldPressure: Use them for pursuit, flank work, and live target acquisition before a scene turns static or heavily scripted.
species.mothershipDescription: The speed apex among natural species. Wraith Raiders react, pursue, and strike before most opponents have processed the danger.
notes: Race Raider is treated as a more feral Wraith Raider variant rather than a separate baseline species template.

specialRules.label: Predator Senses
specialRules.summary: Advantage on Speed-based checks for perception, detecting ambush, or initiative. Never surprised in natural or urban environments unless by supernatural means.

specialRules.label: Natural Weapons
specialRules.summary: Claws deal Strength / 10 + 1d5 damage at Adjacent range and count as primary species weapons.

species.name: Shaktar
species.identity: Saurian warriors of honour and martial tradition, combining brutal strength with disciplined battlefield intent.
species.fieldPressure: Doctrine has to respect their code or the squad risks discipline fractures at the worst possible time.
species.mothershipDescription: A front-line martial species with exceptional Strength, Combat, and Body backed by formidable Sanity and Fear saves.
notes: Tactical and martial intelligence are strong; abstract cognition is weaker than their battlefield presence.

specialRules.label: Honour Bound
specialRules.summary: Breaking a code of honour forces a Sanity Save or the Shaktar gains 1d3 Stress.

specialRules.label: Natural Weapons
specialRules.summary: Claws deal Strength / 10 damage at Adjacent range. Shaktar gain Advantage on grappling or restraining Strength checks.

species.name: Stormer 313 Malice
species.identity: Karma's standard biogenetic combat unit: a massive regenerating killing machine with overwhelming power and minimal subtlety.
species.fieldPressure: They solve breaches, riots, and hard resistance, but subtlety evaporates the moment they become the main plan.
species.mothershipDescription: Near-ceiling assault biology: immense Strength, Combat, and Body with deliberately shallow abstract reasoning and social finesse.
notes: Purpose-built for violence, shock entry, and surviving damage that would kill almost anyone else.

specialRules.label: Prometheus Regeneration
specialRules.summary: At the end of every fourth combat round, recover 1d5 Health. Once wounds are fully healed, continue regenerating 1 Health per round.

specialRules.label: Biogenetic Aggression
specialRules.summary: Disadvantage on Intellect checks involving diplomacy, deception detection, or abstract reasoning.

specialRules.label: Finance Chip Mandatory
specialRules.summary: The Karma finance chip is a built-in hindrance and cannot be removed.

species.name: Stormer 711 Xeno
species.identity: Karma's fast insectile camouflage unit: a stealth biogen built for pursuit, ambush, and pressure from impossible angles.
species.fieldPressure: Best used where speed and feral pressure matter more than conversation, procedure, or sponsor optics.
species.mothershipDescription: Matches Wraith Raider speed with biogen resilience, regeneration, and camouflage systems in place of natural grace.
notes: Fastest movement profile in the game, backed by natural chitin armour and unsettling inhuman presence.

specialRules.label: Chameleon Chitin
specialRules.summary: Spend 1 action to become near-invisible. Detection is at Disadvantage while still; moving breaks the effect.

specialRules.label: Prometheus Regeneration
specialRules.summary: At the end of every fourth combat round, recover 1d5 Health.

specialRules.label: Inhuman Appearance
specialRules.summary: Social Intellect checks against other species are at Disadvantage because the Xeno's appearance is deeply unnerving.

species.name: Advanced Carrien
species.identity: Cunning survivor-mutants with strong instincts and an uneasy fit inside official SLA structures.
species.fieldPressure: They thrive in broken districts and dirty jobs, but prejudice follows them into every briefing room.
species.mothershipDescription: Legacy draft Carrien template retained until a source-grounded 1st Edition conversion pass is written.
notes: This entry is still using the project's legacy Carrien draft because the new conversion notes did not include a Carrien profile.

specialRules.label: Social Prejudice
specialRules.summary: Track district or sponsor prejudice penalties where relevant.
